title: Catamaran Rental Hourly in Puerto Banus: 2026 Costs
html:
Hourly catamaran rental in Puerto Banus lets you board a wide, stable sailing cat at the marina by the Puerto Banus lighthouse and cruise the Marbella coast from roughly EUR 120 to EUR 250 per hour. On BoatHire24 you compare skippered cats by capacity, hour and price before you commit.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

Most hourly catamarans leave from Puerto Jose Banus, the marble-and-glass marina at the western end of Marbella, beneath its landmark lighthouse. Nearby launch and landing points worth knowing include Marina La Bajadilla in Marbella town, the broad sweep of Playa de Nueva Andalucia just west, the sands of San Pedro de Alcantara, and the rocky headland of Cabopino with its small fishing port.

Which boat to rent in Puerto Banus

Hourly hire favours steady, shaded craft that carry a group without crowding, so a sailing catamaran is the natural pick for a relaxed coastal cruise.

  1. Sailing catamaran: best for groups who want shade, space and a flat deck; up to 11 guests; from EUR 150 per hour. You can study the layout, cabins and rates on the Shangani Blue Lagoon 70 ft listing before you book.
  2. Motor catamaran: best for covering more coast quickly while staying level; up to 12 guests; from EUR 180 per hour.
  3. Small motorboat: best for couples or a short spin to a quiet cove; up to 6 guests; from EUR 90 per hour.
  4. RIB: best for a fast, splashy run toward Cabopino; up to 8 guests; from EUR 100 per hour.
  5. Crewed motor yacht: best for a premium hour with a host aboard; up to 10 guests; from EUR 350 per hour.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Puerto Banus?

Expect roughly EUR 90 to EUR 350 per hour in 2026, depending on the size of boat and whether a skipper is aboard. Prices climb in peak summer and ease in the shoulder months. When you want to lock in an hourly slot you can sort live options for boat rental Puerto Banus by date, hour and guest count, then read each price before you pay.

  • A shared coastal tour from Puerto Banus starts around EUR 35 per person for a fixed departure.
  • A small motorboat for a half-day runs from about EUR 350 in EUR.
  • A catamaran for a full day sits in the EUR 900 to EUR 1,800 band.
  • A crewed yacht is typically billed from EUR 2,500 per day, fuel and extras added separately.

When is the best season to rent a catamaran in Puerto Banus?

The high season runs June, July, August and September, when warm, settled days and calm mornings suit hourly catamaran cruising. Spring and early autumn stay pleasant and quieter on the water. The local easterly, the Levante, can build chop and haze through the Strait of Gibraltar, while the westerly Poniente often brings fresher, clearer air. Book early hours to beat the afternoon breeze.

Aim for a morning departure between roughly 10am and noon in July or August: the Mediterranean is glassiest then, the Levante usually has not yet filled in, and you can reach the bay off Nueva Andalucia, around two nautical miles west, before the day-boat traffic peaks.

How does booking an hourly charter actually work?

You choose your date and the number of hours, confirm guest count, and the operator confirms the meeting pontoon and the skipper. A short minimum, often two or three hours, is common for catamarans, and fuel, mooring and any extras are set out before you pay.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 90 to EUR 350 per hour
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Typical trip length: 2 to 4 hours hourly hire
  • Capacity: up to 12 guests on a catamaran
  • Currency: EUR
  • Departure: Puerto Jose Banus marina

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Can you rent a catamaran by the hour in Puerto Banus?

Yes, several sailing and motor catamarans take hourly bookings from Puerto Banus, usually with a two or three hour minimum, and a skipper is included so no qualification is needed from guests.

Do I need a licence to be aboard?

Spain sets the principle that skippered charters carry a qualified operator, so guests aboard a crewed catamaran need no licence; the operator confirms the exact requirements for any boat you wish to drive yourself.

How many people fit on an hourly catamaran?

Most catamarans offered for hourly hire here carry between 8 and 12 guests comfortably, with shaded seating and a flat foredeck, though the precise legal capacity is stated on each individual listing.

What will an hour actually cost?

An hour on a sailing catamaran from Puerto Banus typically starts near EUR 150, with motor cats and crewed yachts costing more, and the live total for your chosen date appears before you confirm.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Puerto Banus or list your own. Compare hourly catamarans, pick your hours and your meeting pontoon, and set off across the Marbella coast knowing the price and the skipper are confirmed in advance for your 2026 trip.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
